Knob.name: ddrmanager_bwl_en
Knob.help: Enable/Disable bandwidth limiting (thermal throttling)

Knob.name: ddrmanager_bwl_polling_en
Knob.help: Enable/Disable DIMM temperature/power polling

Knob.name: ddrmanager_bwl_polling_period_ms
Knob.help: Default DIMM temperature/power polling period in ms

Knob.name: ddrmanager_dimm_temp_low
Knob.help: Integer value representing low temperature to disengage BWL (80°C)

Knob.name: ddrmanager_dimm_temp_high
Knob.help: Integer value representing high temperature to engage BWL (85°C)

Knob.name: ddrmanager_dimm_temp_crit
Knob.help: Integer value representing critical DIMM temperature where bad things happen (95°C)

Knob.name: ddrss_init
Knob.help: DDRSS init enable. Used by DV only, ignored by silibs. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: intu_init_en
Knob.help: DDRSS INTU init enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ddrss_mask
Knob.help: DDRSS enable mask for both dies (12 DDRSSes). Each bit is one DDRSS. (0 ~ 0xFFF)

Knob.name: cfg_profile
Knob.help: DIMM configuration profile for DV only. Please refer to ddrss_cfg_profile_t for encoding. (0 ~ 0xFFFFFFFF)

Knob.name: ddr_speed_grade
Knob.help: DDR Speed Grade

Knob.name: dfi_ratio
Knob.help: DFI to MC frequency ratio. 2 - 1:2 ratio; 4 - 1:4 ratio; Other values are invalid.

Knob.name: rcd_delay
Knob.help: Set DDR PHY RCD delay. (0 ~ 1300, 0xffff means using predefined delay)

Knob.name: data_comp_delay
Knob.help: Set DDR PHY data delay. (0 ~ 1300, 0xffff means using predefined delay)

Knob.name: dimm_sub_ch_swap
Knob.help: DIMM sub-channel swap mode. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable, 2 - Auto (Enable for Die0, Disable for Die1)

Knob.name: dram_init_mode
Knob.help: 0 - Skip DIMM MR and CW init. 1 - Run DIMM CW init, 2 - RUN DIMM MR init, 3 - Run DIMM MR and CW init

Knob.name: interleave_mc_cnt
Knob.help: Number of interleaved MCs. 0 means using all enabled MCs for interleaving. (0 ~ 24)

Knob.name: pas_encryption_en_mask
Knob.help: PAS encryption enabling mask, each bit indicates the encryption enabling state for each PAS (BIT0:S BIT1:NS BIT2:ROOT BIT3:REALM).

Knob.name: address_map_mode
Knob.help: DDR media address map mode. (0 - 4)

Knob.name: fips_kat_en
Knob.help: DDRSS FIPS KAT test enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: perf_debug_profile
Knob.help: DDRSS performance profile select.

Knob.name: dram_refresh_mode
Knob.help: DRAM refersh mode.

Knob.name: sbr_en
Knob.help: DRAM same bank refersh enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: refresh_type_1x
Knob.help: Refresh command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 1x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: refresh_type_2x
Knob.help: Refresh command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 2x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: rfm_type_1x
Knob.help: RFM command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 1x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: rfm_type_2x
Knob.help: RFM command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 2x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: drfm_type_1x
Knob.help: DRFM command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 1x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: drfm_type_2x
Knob.help: DRFM command types permitted to a rank when its refresh rate is 2x. 0 - All Bank 1 - Same Bankand all-bank if required. (Requires master knob sbr_en = 1)

Knob.name: refresh_deficit_threshold
Knob.help: Deficit counter value after which refresh is considered urgent. Valid settings are (0 ~ 3).

Knob.name: refresh_cmd_delay_same_bank
Knob.help: Minimum number of cycles from a same bank command to a subsequent same bank or all bank command. Does not apply to Precharge All and Refresh All Bank Immediate requests. This is counted in media cycles. Specially, value 0xfff means AUTO. (0 ~ 0xfff)

Knob.name: refresh_cmd_delay_all_bank
Knob.help: Minimum number of cycles from an all bank command to a subsequent same bank or all bank command. Does not apply to Precharge All and Refresh All Bank Immediate requests. This is counted in media cycles. Specially, value 0xffff means AUTO. (0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: media_ecc_mode
Knob.help: Media ECC mode.

Knob.name: ras_init_en
Knob.help: Enable RAS initialzation. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ue_max_retry
Knob.help: The number of read retries to do before giving up and flagging an uncorrectable error and marking the page as poisoned. (0 ~ 7)

Knob.name: drop_prefetch_tgt
Knob.help: FECQ prefetch target drop control. BIT0 - Drop new PREFETCHTGT if targeted bank is UNAVAIL; BIT1 - Drop new PREFETCHTGT if current read/write preference is write-only; BIT2 - Drop existing PREFETCHTGT if current read/write preference is write-only. All bits combination are legal. (0 ~ 7)

Knob.name: page_policy
Knob.help: Sets the page policy.

Knob.name: page_open_timer
Knob.help: Number of cycles to wait after there are no cmds to Physical bank in FE before issuing a PRCHG to BE. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: block_pre_conf_cmd_in_be
Knob.help: Block FECQ from issuing PRCHG to BE. 0 - Allow issuing PRCHG, 1 - Block issuing PRCHG

Knob.name: auto_ecs_in_self_refresh_en
Knob.help: Automatic ECS during DRAM self-refresh. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ca_parity_check_en
Knob.help: Enable DRAM command/address bus parity check. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ca_parity_err_recovery_en
Knob.help: CA Parity Error Recovery Procedure upon ALERT_n assertion. 0 - No action, report transient error if CRC or MBIST is disabled; 1 - No recovery, report persistent error; 2 - Attemp to recover. If error re-occurs, report persistent error; 3 - Attemp to recover. If error re-occurs, retry recovery.

Knob.name: manual_ecs_en
Knob.help: Specify the mode that the MC perform DRAM ECS operation. 0 - Use auto-ECS with refresh 1 - Use manual ECS command

Knob.name: address_hashing_mode
Knob.help: Enable Physical Bank hashing on bus/semi address.

Knob.name: sub_bank_hashing_mode
Knob.help: Enable Row Sub-Bank Hashing for row-hammer mitigation.

Knob.name: auto_phy_lp_en
Knob.help: Enable automatic entry of DDR PHY's low power mode. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable when in self-refresh mode, 2 - Enable when in self-refresh or power-down mode, 3 - Enable when in self-refresh or power-down mode, or MC idle

Knob.name: phy_lp_entry_delay
Knob.help: Specify the delay to enter PHY low-power mode once all conditions are satisfied, in multiple-of-8 memory controller cycles. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: phy_lp_wakeup
Knob.help: DFI wakeup parameter. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: auto_phy_lp2_en
Knob.help: Enable automatic entry of the DDR PHY's low power mode, type 2. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: phy_lp2_entry_delay
Knob.help: Specify the delay to enter PHY low-power mode type 2 once all conditions are satisfied, in the time units specified by the delay unit. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: phy_lp2_entry_delay_unit
Knob.help: Specifies the time unit used for entry delay of PHY low-power mode type 2

Knob.name: auto_self_refresh_en
Knob.help: Enable automatic entry of DRAM self-refresh mode. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: temp_comp_refresh_en
Knob.help: Enable periodic Temp Compensation operation. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: periodic_ecs_readout_en
Knob.help: Periodic ECS readout enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: periodic_zq_cal_en
Knob.help: Periodic ZQ cal enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: periodic_osc_cal_en
Knob.help: Periodic DQS osc cal enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: periodic_phy_update
Knob.help: Periodic PHY update enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: periodic_ecs_en
Knob.help: Periodic ecs enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: self_refresh_entry_delay
Knob.help: Delay time for entering DRAM self-refresh mode after entry conditions are satisfied, in the units specified by the delay unit. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: self_refresh_entry_delay_unit
Knob.help: Specifies the time unit used for self-refresh entry delay

Knob.name: dynamic_clk_gating_en
Knob.help: Enable dynamic clock gating for MC and DFI clock. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: dram_active_power_down_en
Knob.help: Specify whether active power down is allowed. 0 - Always use precharged power down mode only, 1 - Allow both active power-down mode and precharge power-down mode are used

Knob.name: dram_power_down_en
Knob.help: Enable automatic entry of DRAM power-down mode. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: dram_power_down_entry_delay
Knob.help: Delay time for entering DRAM power down mode after entry conditions are satisfied. In multiple-of-8 memory controller cycles. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: keystore_scrub_en
Knob.help: Enable keystore patrol scrubbing. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: media_scrub_en
Knob.help: Enable media patrol scrubbing. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: media_scrub_interval
Knob.help: Media patrol scrub interval in unit specified by media_scrub_interval_unit. 0: AUTO interval, 1 ~ 255: customized interval

Knob.name: media_scrub_interval_unit
Knob.help: Media patrol scrub interval unit

Knob.name: phy_train_max_retry
Knob.help: Controls the max number of retry attempts in case of a DDRSS PHY training failure. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: phy_hdt_ctrl
Knob.help: Controls the total number of debug messages. 0x04 - Maximal debug messages; 0x05 - Detailed debug messages; 0x0A - Coarse debug messages; 0xC8 - Stage completion; 0xC9 - Assertion messages; 0xFF - Firmware completion messages only; Invalid for all other values.

Knob.name: phy_qcs_qca_trn_en
Knob.help: Enable PHY FW QCS/QCA training. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: phy_adv_training_opt
Knob.help: Some Advanced Training Options. Please refer to PMU_SMB_DDR5R_1D_t definition in DDR5 PHY release package for details. (0 ~ 0xFF)

Knob.name: phy_more_adv_training_opt
Knob.help: Some more advanced training options. Please refer to PMU_SMB_DDR5R_1D_t definition in DDR5 PHY release package for details. (0 ~ 0xFF)

Knob.name: phy_margin_th
Knob.help: Minimum margin threshold to pass training. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: dq_margin_th
Knob.help: Minimum per lane DQ margin threshold to pass training. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: dq_margin_extract_pattern_len
Knob.help: Controls the pattern length used for per-lane margins extraction. Pattern length is defined as (2 ^ n) * 2048 bits. n = (0 ~ 23)

Knob.name: dq_margin_extract_step_size
Knob.help: Controls the step size of the 1D scans during per-lane margins extraction. Step size is defined as (2 ^ n). n = (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: dq_margin_extract_pattern_seed
Knob.help: PRBS31 pattern initial seed used for per-lane margins extraction. (0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: ppr_type
Knob.help: This value indicates the kind of PPR to be performed

Knob.name: ppr_defect_list
Knob.help: List that contains info on the defect addresses for PPR operation

Knob.name: rfm_en
Knob.help: Enable Refresh managment 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: erhm_en
Knob.help: Enable Enhanced Row Hammer mitigation logic 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable, 2 - AUTO (Enable if DIMM requests)

Knob.name: rfm_raaimt_ovr
Knob.help: Override RFM RAA Initial Management Threshold (0 ~ 15) , 0 - No override: raaimt_ovr,raammt_ovr and raa_dec_ref_val_ovr will be ignored, 1 - override the values

Knob.name: rfm_raammt_ovr
Knob.help: Override RFM RAA Maximum Management Threshold (0 ~ 7)

Knob.name: rfm_raa_dec_ref_ovr
Knob.help: Override RFM RAA counter Decrement per Refresh command (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: ref_temp_changed
Knob.help: Refresh rates and temperatures at which the mediaRefTempChanged interrupt is generated. All bits combinations are legal. (0x00 ~ 0x3F) BIT1 - 1x Refresh Rate, below 80C nominal. BIT2 - 1x Refresh Rate, 80-85C nominal. BIT3 - 2x Refresh Rate, 85-90C nominal. BIT4 - 2x Refresh Rate, 90-95C nominal. BIT5 - 2x Refresh Rate, above 95C nominal.

Knob.name: ref_temp_high
Knob.help: Refresh rate and temperature at which the mediaRefTempHigh interrupt is generated. (1 ~ 5) 1 - 1x Refresh Rate, below 80C nominal. 2 - 1x Refresh Rate, 80-85C nominal. 3 - 2x Refresh Rate, 85-90C nominal. 4 - 2x Refresh Rate, 90-95C nominal. 5 - 2x Refresh Rate, above 95C nominal.

Knob.name: refresh_cmd_delay_ecs_disable
Knob.help: Disable refresh lockout timers for ECS. 0 - Enable, 1 - Disable

Knob.name: refresh_cmd_delay_rfm_disable
Knob.help: Disable refresh lockout timers for RFM. 0 - Enable, 1 - Disable

Knob.name: refresh_cmd_delay_drfm_disable
Knob.help: Disable refresh lockout timers for DRFM. 0 - Enable, 1 - Disable

Knob.name: allow_non_urgent_ref
Knob.help: Allow a non-urgent refresh. (0 ~ 2) 0 - The FECQ has no commands that may require a media access. 1 - The FECQ has no commands that may require a media access to the physical banks targeted by the refresh. 2 - All physical bank targeted by the refresh are closed.

Knob.name: ref_same_bank_order
Knob.help: Same Bank Refresh Order. 0 - Bank order shall be 0, 1, 2, 3 1 - Any bank order is permitted

Knob.name: rm_tm_rec_mask
Knob.help: Firmware telemetry data report mask. (0 ~ 0xffffffff) Each bit dedices the related info should be reported in RHM telemetry record. 0: Exclude the info 1: Include the info BIT0: lower_row; BIT1: upper_row; BIT2: bank; BIT3: bg; BIT4: rank; BIT5: smc; BIT6: soc; BIT7: spill_count; BIT8: way_info_addr; BIT9: way_info_count; BIT10: way_info_lock; BIT24: Telemetry type - Parity error only; BIT25: Telemetry type - DRFM requested; BIT26: Telemetry type - DRFM dropped; BIT27: Telemetry type - Enter or reenter sampling mode; BIT28: Telemetry type - Enter heavy hitters mode;

Knob.name: rm_tm_filter_sub_bank_mask
Knob.help: Sub-banks for which filtered RHM telemetry shall be collected. All bit combinations are legal. Each bit indicate a sub-bank. (0x0000 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: rm_tm_filter_bank_mask
Knob.help: Bank groups for which filtered RHM telemetry shall be collected. All bit combinations are legal. Each bit indicate a bank. (0x00 ~ 0x0f)

Knob.name: rm_tm_filter_bg_mask
Knob.help: Bank groups for which filtered RHM telemetry shall be collected. All bit combinations are legal. Each bit indicate a bank group. (0x00 ~ 0xff)

Knob.name: rm_tm_filter_rank_mask
Knob.help: Ranks for which filtered RHM telemetry shall be collected. All bit combinations are legal. Each bit indicate a rank. (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: rm_tm_sel_always
Knob.help: Types of RHM telemetry events to always collect.

Knob.name: rm_tm_sel_filtered
Knob.help: Types of RHM telemetry events to collect with filter.

Knob.name: rm_drt_clr
Knob.help: RHM DRFM Row Tracking structure clear rate. Number of 1.95us pulses.

Knob.name: rm_cnt_clr
Knob.help: RHM counter's structure clear rate in number of 1.95us pulses.

Knob.name: rm_sample_thr
Knob.help: RHM sampling probability. Must be greater than 2.

Knob.name: rm_soc
Knob.help: RHM sampling Overflow Countdown initial value.

Knob.name: rm_smc_1
Knob.help: RHM sampling Mode Countdown initial value.

Knob.name: rm_smc_2
Knob.help: RHM sampling Mode Countdown initial value randomizing mask.

Knob.name: rm_act_thr
Knob.help: RHM HH (Heavy Hitters) activate counter threshold. Must be greater than 2.

Knob.name: be_cmd_exec_opt
Knob.help: The command execution options for the back end. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: be_cmd_select_weight
Knob.help: The weight of each DRAM command type that is used in the back end command arbitration. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: bump_priority_level
Knob.help: Priority Bump Configuration. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_chi_qos_to_prio_rd
Knob.help: The mapping from CHI QOS field to the command priority for the read, prefetch and cache maintenance operations used in the Bus Interface logic. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_chi_qos_to_prio_wr
Knob.help: The mapping from CHI QOS field to the command priority for the write operations used in the Bus Interface logic. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_cob_skip_cnt
Knob.help: The FECQ enqueue skip counts for each command priority, at and above which the command in the COB slice is considered starved. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_drop_pftgt_fecq_occ
Knob.help: The occupancy level at and above which a PrefetchTarget command will be dropped in the Bus Interface logic, for each command priority. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_pcrdgrant_skip_cnt
Knob.help: The RetryAck response skip counts for each command priority, at and above which an internal PCrdGrant response request is considered as starved. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: busif_rcb_skip_cnt
Knob.help: The COB enqueue skip counts for each command priority, at and above which the RCB command is considered starved. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: en_data_parity_check
Knob.help: Enable Data Parity Check. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: en_cmd_retry
Knob.help: Enable Command Retry. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: async_cmd_fifo_en
Knob.help: Bus Interface Asynchronous Command FIFO address hash enable: 00 - less occupied; 01 - pipe 0 only; 10 - pipe 1 only; 11 - hash. (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: semi_dest_pipe_en
Knob.help: Enable SEMI Destination Pipe: 0 illegal ; 1 CommandPipe0 ; 2 CommandPipe1 ; 3 CommandPipe0or1. (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: semi_cmd_prio
Knob.help: Enable SEMI Command Priority: 0 BusCmds ; 1 SemiCmds. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: pa_trace_stall
Knob.help: PA Trace Stall. Determines what occurs if a command is marked for PA tracing and the PA Trace FIFO is full. 0: The command is dispatched and not traced. 1: Dispatch of the command is stalled until it can be traced. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: pcrdgrant_delay
Knob.help: Maximum MC cycles between PCrdGrant responses. Used to prioritize a PCrdGrant response over a RetryAck response if the PCrdGrant would not otherwise be sent to one of the Bus Interface Response FIFOs. Programmable over a range of 0 to 7 MC cycles. A delay of 0 corresponds to back-to-back PCrdGrant responses. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: skip_req_exists_pcrdgrant
Knob.help: Specifies if a PCrdGrant must be pending for the PCrdGrant starvation logic to consider a Bus Interface command priority level starved. 0: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented when a PCrdGrant is sent. 1: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented only when a PCrdGrant is pending for Bus Interface command priority level n and a PCrdGrant is sent. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: skip_req_exists_rcb
Knob.help: Specifies if a command must exist in the RCB to COB slice x at Bus Interface command priority n for the starvation lgoic to consider Bus Interface command priority level n starved for COB slice x. 0: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented if it is non-zero when a command is enqueued into COB slice x. 1: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented if it is non-zero only when a command exists in the RCB to COB slice x at Bus Interface command priority n and a command is enqueued into COB slice x. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: skip_req_exists_cob
Knob.help: Specifies if a command must exist in a COB slice at Bus Interface command priority n for the starvation logic to consider Bus Interface command priority levle n starved for that COB slice. 0: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented if it is non-zero when a command is enqueued into the associated FECQ slice. 1: Starvation countdown for Bus Interface command priority n is decremented if it is non-zero only when a command exists in the COB slice to Bus Interface command priority n and a command is enqueued into the associated FECQ slice. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: rcb_en
Knob.help: Enables RCB. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: addr_err_report
Knob.help: Enables Address Error Report. 0: No NDERR no RAS report; 1: Return NDERR and RAS report. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: async_cmd_fifo_depth
Knob.help: Bus Interface Asynchronous command FIFO Depth. Programmable over a range of 1 to the FIFOs physical depth. (0x0 ~ 0x1f)

Knob.name: rcb_depth
Knob.help: Bus Interface Retried Command Buffer Depth. Programmable over a range of 1 to the RCBs physical depth. (0 ~ 59)

Knob.name: cob_slice_depth
Knob.help: Bus Interface Command Overflow Buffer Slice Depth. Programmable over a range of 1 to COB Slices physical Depth. (0x0 ~ 0x7f)

Knob.name: fecq_slice_depth
Knob.help: FECQ Slice Depth. Programmable over a range of 1 to an FECQ Slices physical depth. (0x0 ~ 0x1f)

Knob.name: resp_fifo_depth
Knob.help: Bus Interface Response FIFO Depth. Programmable over a range of 1 to the Bus Interface Response FIFOs physical depth. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: chi_cbusy_map_mpam_0
Knob.help: The mapping for CHI CBusy field from the command priority and FECQ congestion level. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: chi_cbusy_map_mpam_1
Knob.help: The mapping for CHI CBusy field from the FECQ congestion level and MPAM bandwidth for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: chi_cbusy_map_mpam_2
Knob.help: The mapping for CHI CBusy field from the FECQ congestion level and MPAM bandwidth for command priority 1. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: chi_cbusy_map_mpam_3
Knob.help: The mapping for CHI CBusy field from the FECQ congestion level and MPAM bandwidth for command priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: chi_cbusy_map_mpam_4
Knob.help: The mapping for CHI CBusy field from the FECQ congestion level and MPAM bandwidth for command priority 3. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: cob_occ_limit_prio0
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface COB occupancy levels for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: cob_occ_limit_prio1
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface COB occupancy levels for command priority 1. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: cob_occ_limit_prio2
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface COB occupancy levels for command priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: cob_occ_limit_prio3
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface COB occupancy levels for command priority 3. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: dp_busrdfifo_depth
Knob.help: BusRd Async FIFO depth CSR for DP to busif to throttle tokens 0 - invalid, 4 - 4 entries, 5 - 5 entries, ... ..., 15 - 15 entries. (1 ~ 15)

Knob.name: fedb_busrdfifo_depth
Knob.help: FEDB to BusRd deferred read FIFO depth CSR to throttle tokens 0 - invalid, 4 - 4 entries, 5 - 5 entries, ... ..., 7 - 7 entries. (1 ~ 7)

Knob.name: self_refresh_with_ck_stop
Knob.help: Specify whether DRAM clock can be stopped during self-refresh mode 0 - DRAM clock continues to run during self-refresh 1 - DRAM clock can be stopped during self-refresh. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: fecq_bandwidth_limiter_config
Knob.help: FECQ Bandwidth Limiter for Thermal Throttling. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_becq_pri_map_config
Knob.help: FECQ BECQ Command Priority Mapping 2'b00: BECQ Priority 0 2'b01: BECQ Priority 1 2'b10: BECQ Priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_cmd_dispatch_limit_config
Knob.help: FECQ Command Dispatch Limit Threshold. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_congest_lvl_0
Knob.help: The thresholds to determine the FECQ occupancy congestion level for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_congest_lvl_1
Knob.help: The thresholds to determine the FECQ occupancy congestion level for command priority 1. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_congest_lvl_2
Knob.help: The thresholds to determine the FECQ occupancy congestion level for command priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_congest_lvl_3
Knob.help: The thresholds to determine the FECQ occupancy congestion level for command priority 3. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_congest_lvl_alt
Knob.help: The alternate thresholds to determine the FECQ occupancy congestion level for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_occ_limit_prio0
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the FECQ occupancy levels for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_occ_limit_prio1
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the FECQ occupancy levels for command priority 1. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_occ_limit_prio2
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the FECQ occupancy levels for command priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_occ_limit_prio3
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the FECQ occupancy levels for command priority 3. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: fecq_over_subscr_total_occ
Knob.help: Total FECQ oversubscription occupancy. (0x0 ~ 0xff)

Knob.name: fecq_over_subscr_slice_occ
Knob.help: FECQ Slice oversubscription occupancy. (0x0 ~ 0x1f)

Knob.name: criteria
Knob.help: FECQ occupancy criteria used to determine when the MC is oversubscribed for the purposes of mapping a commands Bus Interface command priority to FECQ priority. 0: MC will never be considered oversubscribed. 1: Target FECQ Slice occupancy is at least the SLICE_OCC in this register. 2: Any FECQ Slice occupancy is at least the SLICE_OCC in this register. 4: Total FECQ occupancy is at least 5: Total FECQ occupancy is at least the TOTAL_OCC in this register or target FECQ Slice occupancy is at least the SLICE_OCC in this register. the TOTAL_OCC in this register. 6: Total FECQ occupancy is at least the TOTAL_OCC in this register or any FECQ Slice occupancy is at least the SLICE_OCC in this register. (0 ~ 7)

Knob.name: fecq_rdpriignorepref
Knob.help: Bit vector indicating FECQ priority levels at which read requests can be sent to the BE or request precharges regardless of the current read/write preference. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: fecq_rdpriprefrd
Knob.help: If true, a read that sends a command to the BE or request a precharge when only writes are permitted switches the preference to reads only when the criteria specified by fecqStartWrPref is not satisfied. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: fecq_wrpriignorepref
Knob.help: Bit vector indicating FECQ priority levels at which write requests can be sent to the BE or request precharges regardless of the current read/write preference. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: fecq_wrpriprefwr
Knob.help: If true, a write that sends a command to the BE or request a precharge when only reads are permitted switches the preference to writes only when the criteria specified by fecqStopWrPref is not satisfied. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: send_wr_max_cycle
Knob.help: Maximum number of media cycles the FECQ is permitted to send only writes to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0xfff)

Knob.name: send_wr_max_idle_cycles
Knob.help: Maximum number of idle media cycles between two back-to-back writes. (0x0 ~ 0xff)

Knob.name: send_rd_max_cycles
Knob.help: Maximum number of media cycles the FECQ is permitted to send only READS to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0xfff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_minfecqrd_count
Knob.help: Required number of ready reads in the FECQ to allow only reads or only writes. (0x0 ~ 0x1ff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_minfecqwr_count
Knob.help: Required number of ready writes in the FECQ to allow only reads only writes. (0x0 ~ 0x1ff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_minfecq_count
Knob.help: Required number of ready reads plus ready writes in the FECQ to allow only reads or only writes. (0x0 ~ 0x1ff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_basicmode
Knob.help: Read/Write Preference Mode. 3'b000 - FECQ Read/Write Preference Disabled. 3'b001 - FECQ ready read count exceeds rdWrPrefMinFecqRdCount. 3'b010 - FECQ ready write count exceeds rdWrPrefMinFecqWrCount. 3'b011 - FECQ ready read count exceeds rdWrPrefMinFecqRdCount or FECQ ready write count exceeds rdWrPrefMinFecqWrCount. 3'b111 - FECQ ready read plus ready write count exceeds rdWrPrefMinFecqCount.

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_startwrpref
Knob.help: Start Writes Mode. 2'b00 - Disabled. 2'b01 - Any FECQ slice ready write count is above fecqSliceStartWrCount. 2'b10 - FECQ ready write count is above fecqStartWrCount. 2'b11 - Any FECQ slice read write count is above fecqSliceStartWrCount or the FECQ ready write count is above fecqStartWrCount.

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_stopwrpref
Knob.help: Stop Writes Mode 2'b00 - Disabled, likely not needed. 2'b01 - All FECQ slice ready write counts are below fecqSliceStopWrCount. 2'b10 - FECQ ready write count is below fecqStopWrCount. 2'b11 - All FECQ slice ready write count is below fecqSliceStopWrCount and FECQ ready write count is below fecqStartWrCount.

Knob.name: fecq_fecqslicestartwr_count
Knob.help: Number of ready writes in ANY FECQ slice that causes the Read/Write Preference Logic to start sending only writes to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0x3f)

Knob.name: fecq_fecqslicestopwr_count
Knob.help: Number of ready writes in ALL FECQ slices that causes the Read/Write Preference Logic to stop sending only writes to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0x1ff)

Knob.name: fecq_startwr_count
Knob.help: Number of ready writes in the FECQ that causes the Read/Write Preference Logic to start sending only writes to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0x1ff)

Knob.name: fecq_stopwr_count
Knob.help: Number of ready writes in the FECQ that causes the Read/Write Preference Logic to stop sending only writes to the BE. (0x0 ~ 0xff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_wr_time_out
Knob.help: Maximum number of media cycles between two back-to-back writes when a sendable writes exists and the Read/Write preference logic has been continuously allowing only reads. If this limit is reached, only writes shall be permitted until the sendWrMaxCycles or sendWrMaxIdleCycles limit is reached. When this field is programmed to zero this feature is disabled. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: fecq_rdwrpref_send_rd_max_idle_cycles
Knob.help: Maximum number of idle media cycles between two back-to-back reads. (0x0 ~ 0xff)

Knob.name: fedb_ccdb_num_wr2dp_cmd
Knob.help: Configure the number of entries in the Common Command Data Buffer (CCDB) that can be used for accepting commands to send FEDB data to the Write Data Path (media). The value of the 4-bit field indicates the no. of entries. 4'b000 -> 0 entries ... 4'b1000 -> 8 entries. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: fedb_ccdb_num_lcl_cmd
Knob.help: Configure the number of entries in the Common Command Data Buffer (CCDB) that can be used for accepting local commands (commands other than write to BE). The value of the 4-bit field indicates the no. of entries that must be alloted for these. 4'b000 -> 0 entries ... 4'b1000 -> 8 entries. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: fedb_ccdb_num_total_cmd
Knob.help: Configure the Total number of entries in the Common Command Data Buffer (CCDB) that can be used for accepting 'any' commands. This is used for power/performance analysis and supercedes the numwr2dp and numlcl. The value of the 4-bit field indicates the no. of entries. 4'b000 -> 0 entries ... 4'b1000 -> 8 entries. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: lockout_priority_threshold
Knob.help: The priority theshold above which the dispatch lockout timers will not be applied. This is a 1-hot field. High bit set indicates the highest priority. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: lockout_priority_enable
Knob.help: Enables the prioity threshold lockout featues. 0 - disabled, no priority threshold will be applied to the dispatch lockout timers. 1 - enabled, priority threshold will be applied to the dispatch lockout timers. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_mbw_min_overflow_max
Knob.help: Number of bandwidth accounting windows worth of bandwidth in excess of a PARTID's minimum bandwidth limit to be tracked by the logic. Must not be programmed to zero. (1 ~ 255)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_mbw_max_overflow_max
Knob.help: Number of bandwidth accounting windows worth of bandwidth in excess of a PARTID's maximum bandwidth limit to be tracked by the logic. Must not be programmed to zero. (1 ~ 255)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_bm_dis
Knob.help: MPAM Bandwidth Monitoring Disable. Set to 1'b1 to disable the MPAM Bandwidth Monitoring Logic. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_bp_dis
Knob.help: MPAM Bandwidth Partitioning Disable. Set to 1'b1 to disable the MPAM Bandwidth Partitioning Logic. (0 ~ 1)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_winwd
Knob.help: Contents are identical to MPAMCFG_MBW_WINWD. (0x0 ~ 0xffffff)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_mbw_xfers
Knob.help: Number of media requests that can be issued in the amount of time specified by MPAM_MBW_WINWD. This must be programmed prior to configuring PARTID bandwidths via MPAM_MBW_MIN[MIN] or MPAM_MBW_MAX[MAX]. (0x0 ~ 0x3fff)

Knob.name: mc_mpam_mbw_cycles
Knob.help: Contents are identical to MPAMCFG_MBW_WINWD. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: cmd_merge_en
Knob.help: Command merge enable config. 2'b00: When a command completes it invalidates. No data sharing is permitted. All prefetchTgt commands shall be dropped. 2'b01: When a command other that prefetchTgt completes, it is invalidated. 2'b10: When permitted, a newer comamnd may reuse data from an older command. All merges are allowed. (0 ~ 3)

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_ns_max
Knob.help: Non-secure state memory maximum bandwidth allocated to the partition selected by MPAMCFG_PART_SEL. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_ns_hardlim
Knob.help: Non-secure state hard bandwidth limiting. 0 - When MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the partition contends with a low preference for downstream bandwidth beyond MAX. 1 - When MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the partition does not use any more bandwidth until the memory bandwidth measurement for the partition falls below MAX.

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_s_max
Knob.help: Secure state memory maximum bandwidth allocated to the partition selected by MPAMCFG_PART_SEL. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_s_hardlim
Knob.help: Secure state hard bandwidth limiting. 0 - When MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the partition contends with a low preference for downstream bandwidth beyond MAX. 1 - When MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the partition does not use any more bandwidth until the memory bandwidth measurement for the partition falls below MAX.

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_ns_min
Knob.help: Non-secure state memory minimum bandwidth allocated to the partition selected by MPAMCFG_PART_SEL. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: mpam_mbw_s_min
Knob.help: Secure state memory minimum bandwidth allocated to the partition selected by MPAMCFG_PART_SEL. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: patrol_scrub_urgency_qos
Knob.help: QOS Priority Level. (0 ~ 15)

Knob.name: prio_map_to_fecq
Knob.help: The mapping from the Bus Interface command priority to the FECQ priority. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: rcb_occ_limit_prio0
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface RCB occupancy levels for command priority 0. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: rcb_occ_limit_prio1
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface RCB occupancy levels for command priority 1. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: rcb_occ_limit_prio2
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface RCB occupancy levels for command priority 2. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: rcb_occ_limit_prio3
Knob.help: The criteria to determine the Bus Interface RCB occupancy levels for command priority 3. (0x0 ~ 0xffffffff)

Knob.name: semi_cmd_resp_limit
Knob.help: Total commands that can have all responses outstanding. (0x0 ~ 0x1f)

Knob.name: self_refresh_threshold
Knob.help: SEMI will take media out of self-refresh if deficit goes over this threshold. (0x0 ~ 0xffff)

Knob.name: ecc_ce_th
Knob.help: ECC threshold count after which any per-rank CE counter creates an appropriate RAS error record. (0x0 ~ 0x7ff)

Knob.name: err_cnt_leak_rate
Knob.help: Common leak rate and enable control for all counters. 0: Leak is disabled. Others: Leak is enabled with specified rate. (0x0 ~ 0x1ffffffffffff)

Knob.name: phy_zcal_interval
Knob.help: PHY ZCAL interval between successive calibrations. 0: continuous; 3: 1ms; 4: 2ms; 5: 3ms; 6: 4ms; 7: 8ms; 8: 10ms; 9: 20ms.

Knob.name: ecs_readout_interval
Knob.help: Periodic ECS readout interval value in DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT. 0 : 256 units; 1 to 255 : number of units.

Knob.name: ecs_readout_interval_unit
Knob.help: Periodic ECS readout interval unit.

Knob.name: zq_cal_interval
Knob.help: Periodic ZQCAL interval value in DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT. 0 : 256 units; 1 to 255 : number of units.

Knob.name: zq_cal_interval_unit
Knob.help: Periodic ZQCAL interval unit (DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT).

Knob.name: osc_cal_interval
Knob.help: Periodic OSCCAL interval value in DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT. 0 : 256 units; 1 to 255 : number of units.

Knob.name: osc_cal_interval_unit
Knob.help: Periodic OSCCAL interval unit (DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT).

Knob.name: temp_read_interval
Knob.help: Periodic temp readout interval value in DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT. 0 : 256 units; 1 to 255 : number of units.

Knob.name: temp_read_interval_unit
Knob.help: Periodic temp readout interval unit (DDRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT).

Knob.name: ctrlupd_interval
Knob.help: Periodic DFI controller-initiated updates interval in DRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT. 0 : 256 units; 1 to 255 : number

Knob.name: ctrlupd_interval_unit
Knob.help: Periodic periodic DFI controller-initiated updates interval unit (DRSS_INTERVAL_TIME_UNIT).

Knob.name: ctrlupd_mode
Knob.help: Specify the controller condition in which the DFI update is performed. 0 - Update when each memory rank is either idle or in power-down or self-refresh state. 1 - Update when all memory ranks are in self-refresh mode.

Knob.name: phy_fw_diag_en
Knob.help: DDR PHY firmware diagnostic enable (DV debug only, FW halts after PHY init). 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ue_scrub_poison_en
Knob.help: Enable a poison write to the media caused by a patrol scrub read on a persistent UE. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ue_demand_poison_en
Knob.help: Enable a poison write to the media caused by a demand read on a persistent UE. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: ddrss_ras_fw_first
Knob.help: Entire DDRSS FW-first RAS flow enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: pll_bypass_en
Knob.help: DDRSS PLL programming is disabled and PLL is in bypass 0 - Disable (plls are programmed), 1 - Enable (pll are in bypass)

Knob.name: inband_crc_mode
Knob.help: DDRSS inband CRC mode. 0: Disable 1: CRC Enable for RD only 2: CRC Enable for WR only 3: CRC Enable for RD and WR

Knob.name: dqs_interval_timer_run_time
Knob.help: Specifies how long the DRAM oscillator runs once started in a calibration, In multiple-of-16 memory controller clock cycles. 0: 8192 cycles; 1: 16 cycles; ...; 254: 4064 cycles; 255: 4096 cycles. (0 ~ 255)

Knob.name: pll_frac_en
Knob.help: DDRSS PLL fraction mode enable. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable

Knob.name: bitrot_TCM_delay
Knob.help: Delay after reading TCM memory types in ms.

Knob.name: ddr_debug_level
Knob.help: Control DDR debug print level. 0: None; 1: Critical; 2: Info; 3: Debug; 4:Trace. (0 ~ 4)

Knob.name: parallel_phy_train_en
Knob.help: Run DDR PHY training FW in parallel. 0 - Disable, 1 - Enable
